name: Trail of the Hoyal Maidens (PRC-P-19)
name_original: Las Doncellas del Hoyal (PRC-P-19) — circular por la Laguna Corazón
description: This moderate 6.6-mile hike in Montaña Palentina features the heart-shaped Laguna Corazón and wooden sculptures from local legends.
region_intro: Hiking around Guardo offers access to the varied landscapes of the Palencia mountains, characterized by river valleys, forested areas, and rolling hills. The region features a mix of moderate ascents and flatter sections, providing diverse terrain for outdoor activities. Trails often follow natural waterways and lead through woodlands, showcasing the local flora and geological formations. Best hiking trails around Guardo The most popular hiking route is Trail of the Hoyal Maidens (PRC-P-19), a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through natural landscapes, offering a balanced hiking experience. Another top favourite among local hikers is Monte Corcos and Ermita del Cristo Trail, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This trail includes ascents to Monte Corcos and passes by the Ermita del Cristo, providing views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Senda del Roblón del Monasterio (PRC-P 12) — circular desde Velilla del Río Carrión, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail leading through forested areas and along the Río Carrión, often completed in about 4 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Guardo is defined by river valleys, ancient woodlands, and moderate hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Guardo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 120 hikers have used komoot to explore Guardo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Guardo?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Guardo, offering a variety of experiences. These include 8 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 1 more challenging option, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Guardo?
A: Hikes around Guardo feature the varied landscapes of the Palencia mountains, including river valleys, forested areas, and rolling hills. You'll find a mix of moderate ascents and flatter sections, often following natural waterways and leading through woodlands.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Guardo offers 8 easy hiking routes. A good option for a relaxed walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31845133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velilla del Río Carrión loop from Velilla del Río Carrión&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.5 km long with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for most abilities.

Q: How long do hikes around Guardo typically take?
A: The duration of hikes varies. Shorter, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31845133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velilla del Río Carrión loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092518050/trail-of-the-hoyal-maidens-prc-p-19" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Hoyal Maidens (PRC-P-19)&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 3 hours. Longer moderate routes can extend to 4-5 hours.

Q: Are there any circular walks in the Guardo area?
A: Yes, several circular walks are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103899978/senda-del-roblon-del-monasterio-prc-p-12-circular-desde-velilla-del-rio-carrion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Roblón del Monasterio (PRC-P 12)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 16.1 km circular route through forested areas. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24633521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roblón of El Monasterio loop from Velilla del Río Carrión&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12 km long.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Many trails offer scenic views of the Palencia mountains. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5200388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roblón of El Monasterio&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient oak tree, which is featured on several routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104254666/monte-corcos-and-ermita-del-cristo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Corcos and Ermita del Cristo Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides views from Monte Corcos and passes by the Ermita del Cristo.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Guardo?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Guardo are generally spring and autumn, when the weather is mild and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, though some days might be warmer. Winter hiking is possible, but some higher elevation trails may be affected by snow or colder conditions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Guardo?
A: The trails in Guardo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the quiet woodlands, the diverse terrain, and the well-maintained paths that showcase the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any nearby attractions or places to explore after a hike?
A: Yes, there are several interesting places nearby. You could visit the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5793535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velilla del Río Carrión&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5543242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Almuhey&lt;/a&gt;, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5649404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Brezo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead isn't always detailed, many popular hiking areas and villages in the Palencia mountains, including those near Guardo, typically offer designated parking spaces or roadside parking options. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Guardo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Guardo area, especially if kept on a leash. It's always a good idea to be mindful of local regulations, especially in protected natural areas or near livestock. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Where does the Las Doncellas del Hoyal trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route that starts and ends in the village of Muñeca, which is part of the municipality of Guardo. There is parking available near the trailhead in Muñeca, making it convenient to access.

faqs.question: Is the Las Doncellas del Hoyal trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered to have a moderate difficulty level. While it's accessible for most, a good physical condition is recommended for some sections. It has little technical difficulty, making it manageable for hikers with some experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse mix of terrain, including paths, unpaved tracks, and some shorter sections of paved track and road. The route traverses meadows, oak and holm oak groves, and rocky areas, showcasing both Atlantic and Mediterranean vegetation. You'll also see ancient stone walls and remnants of past mining activity.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: A significant highlight is the Corazón de Muñeca Lagoon, an artificial lake named for its distinctive heart shape. You'll also discover numerous wooden sculptures depicting characters from the local "Legend of Las Doncellas del Hoyal," adding a unique cultural element to your walk. The trail also passes by other small lagoons and offers spectacular views of the Montaña Palentina.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Las Doncellas del Hoyal trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beauty throughout the year, but spring and autumn are often ideal due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, but be prepared for warmer weather. Winter hikes are possible but may require appropriate gear for colder conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Las Doncellas del Hoyal trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas where wildlife may be present, dogs are generally welcome. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and comfort of other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail or visit Montaña Palentina?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the Las Doncellas del Hoyal trail. It is freely accessible to the public. The Montaña Palentina region, where the trail is located, does not typically require permits for general hiking activities on designated trails.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. You might spot diverse flora and fauna, including foxes, deer, and various birds of prey. Keep an eye out for signs of larger mammals like wolves, though they are more elusive.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. Approximately 14% of the trail follows part of the "Camino Olvidado (Matamorisca-Puente Almuhey)", and about 3% runs along "Calle la Fuentona".

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Muñeca or Guardo?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail starts in Muñeca, which is part of the larger municipality of Guardo. Both villages offer some local services. Guardo, being a larger town, will have more options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation if you plan to stay in the area before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and diverse terrain, we recommend bringing sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing layers for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also advisable, although the trail is well-signaled. Don't forget your camera for the scenic views and unique sculptures!

waypoints.name: Corazón de Muñeca Lagoon and Guardo
